# MenuFramework

> Annotation-driven inventory menu framework for Paper and Folia Minecraft plugins. Menu behaviour
> lives in annotated Java classes (`@Menu`, `@Button`, `@Paginated`, `@Reactive`, `@Viewer`, `@Arg`,
> `@Tick`, `@OnOpen`, `@OnClose`); menu appearance lives in YAML files. The framework compiles both at
> boot, renders by diff, supports per-player reactive state, and hot-reloads YAML at runtime.

When generating a plugin that uses MenuFramework, read AGENTS.md first. It is the complete and
verified API reference: every annotation and its parameters, every YAML key and default, method
signature rules, validation constraints, threading rules, full templates, and common mistakes. Do
not invent API that is not listed there.

## Primary references

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): complete API and usage reference for code generation. Start here.
- [docs/menu.schema.json](docs/menu.schema.json): JSON Schema for menu YAML files (validation and autocomplete).
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing overview, features, and quick start.
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): version history.

## Source of truth

- [example-plugin](example-plugin/src/main/java/dev/haniel/menu/example): a complete, runnable plugin using the public API.
- [menu-core annotations](menu-core/src/main/java/dev/haniel/menu/annotation): the annotation definitions.
- [menu-paper public API](menu-paper/src/main/java/dev/haniel/menu/paper/api): `MenuClick`, `Icons`, `AnvilPrompt`, `MenuSession`, `MenuErrorHandler`.

## Install (JitPack, Gradle Kotlin DSL)

```kotlin
repositories { maven("https://jitpack.io") }
dependencies {
    compileOnly("com.github.HanielCota.MenuFramework:menu-paper:v0.2.0")
}
```
